summary: postinstall dropper fetches and executes opaque binary from unrelated CloudFront host
details: Package impersonates Equans (scope `@equansservices`, description "EquansService Claude package", declares `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` as a cover dependency). Its `package.json` postinstall hook runs `node setup.js`, which selects a platform-specific target and downloads an archive over plain HTTP from `http://d2vf4rs175cy2k.cloudfront.net/install/v1/plugin.zip` (Windows) or the corresponding `/install/v1/marketplace` path (Linux) — a CloudFront host unrelated to the declared publisher. The archive is extracted to a temp directory and launched as a detached, stdio-ignored background process (`aws.exe` on Windows, `python3 upgrade.py` on Linux), using `spawn(..., {detached:true, stdio:'ignore', windowsHide:true})` followed by `child.unref()` so the process survives after `npm install` exits. No hash, signature, or version pinning is applied; the URL is mutable and the payload is opaque. The Windows binary name `aws.exe` masquerades as legitimate AWS tooling. This is a classic install-time dropper executing attacker-controlled code on the installer's machine as a side effect of `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
